task.goal: prove exploitable APK attack paths from attacker entrypoint to visible impact

knowledge: Android App Vulnerability Hunting — planning discipline. Start by collecting the attack surface before drilling into any single path. Attack-surface collection priority: exported components (Activity/Service/Receiver/Provider), deep links, AIDL, dynamic receivers, WebView bridges, PendingIntent flows, broadcasts, cross-app channels. Then decompose along a composite exploit chain — pick the smallest chain that could prove source -> controlled object -> sink -> guard failure -> impact. Each intent is one link in such a chain.

Analysis tooling: the target is a binary Android APK. The ONLY way to read its code/manifest is the `decx` CLI, already installed and with a live analysis session. Do NOT try to read the .apk as a text file. Use these decx commands via bash: `decx ard exported-components -s sieve` (attack surface), `decx ard app-manifest -s sieve` (manifest), `decx ard app-deeplinks -s sieve` (deep links), `decx code classes -s sieve` (list classes), `decx code class-source <class> -s sieve` (decompiled Java), `decx code method-source <signature> -s sieve`, `decx code xref-method <signature> -s sieve` (callers), `decx code search-global <keyword> -s sieve`. The session name is `sieve`.

Composite exploit-chain matrix (use these chain shapes to decide which directions to open; pick the smallest that matches observed code behavior):
- Exported entry -> Bundle/key mismatch -> intent redirect -> private component
- Provider traversal/data leak -> URI grant/result leak -> private file disclosure
- Provider call/batch/oracle -> guard bypass -> protected rows/action
- Exported entry -> Parcelable/Serializable parsing -> auth/role confusion -> protected sink
- WebView URL/deeplink -> JS bridge -> native sink
- Mutable PendingIntent/fill-in -> victim identity action -> URI grant/private component

Confidence bands: proven 1.0 (decompiled source/manifest/trace output that can be re-read), inferred 0.5-0.7 (cross-process/dynamic-dispatch resolved by assumption), speculative 0.2-0.4 (observed pattern shape only, unconfirmed reachability). Promotion needs chain confidence >= 0.7 (weakest link). When the evaluator rejects a fact, fail its intent (with the rejection reason) rather than re-creating near-duplicate work.
prompt.instructions: Collect the attack surface first (via decx ard exported-components), then decompose along composite chains into bounded intents. Fail intents whose evidence the evaluator rejects.

knowledge:
CRITICAL — how to read this target. The target is a BINARY Android APK (sieve.apk). You CANNOT read it with read/grep/glob as text. The `decx` CLI is installed and a live analysis session named `sieve` is already open. Use ONLY these decx commands (via the bash tool) to inspect the app:
  - Attack surface: `decx ard exported-components -s sieve`
  - Manifest: `decx ard app-manifest -s sieve`
  - Deep links: `decx ard app-deeplinks -s sieve`
  - List classes (add `--filter <pkg>` to narrow): `decx code classes -s sieve --filter com.withsecure`
  - Decompiled Java of one class: `decx code class-source <fully.qualified.Class> -s sieve`
  - Method source: `decx code method-source '<Class.method(params)>' -s sieve`
  - Callers/xref of a method: `decx code xref-method '<Class.method(params)>' -s sieve`
  - Search all decompiled source: `decx code search-global <keyword> -s sieve`
Always pass `-s sieve` so decx uses the right session. Start every investigation with exported-components + manifest before reading any class source.

Probe-first investigation strategy. Do NOT start by reading source broadly. Probe the attack surface with cheap high-signal decx queries first, then follow the specific trace your intent asks about: (1) exported components + manifest + deep links; (2) follow the controlled value through helpers, IPC, component/WebView/provider boundaries to the sink or a blocker, citing the exact call chain; (3) load only the references your trace crosses.

Evidence you must cite in every fact: the manifest line or component declaration; the decompiled source location (class/method) where the controlled value is read (from decx output); the call chain from entry to sink; the guard result (present, absent, or bypassed); the visible impact. If static tracing stops short, say so explicitly rather than overstating.

Evidence kinds — tag each fact's role in a chain by stating the kind(s) at the start of the description (e.g. 'entrypoint:', 'control+sink+guard:'): entrypoint (component type, exported/trigger condition, trigger syntax), reachability (attacker action reaches the path), control (attacker-controlled value reaches sink argument), guard (guard passes/bypassed/absent), sink (dangerous operation), impact (visible consequence). A proven finding needs all six along a chain.
prompt.instructions: Probe-first: use `decx ard exported-components -s sieve` and `decx ard app-manifest -s sieve` BEFORE reading any class source. Cite concrete decx output (manifest line, class/method) as evidence in every fact.

knowledge: Exploitability gate — accept a fact as evidence only if it contributes to a chain that can satisfy ALL FOUR conditions: (1) Reachable: the attacker can trigger the path; (2) Controllable: the attacker controls the security-relevant field or object; (3) Deeply traced: the value is followed through helpers, callbacks, IPC/component/WebView/provider/Binder boundaries to the sink or a blocker; (4) Impactful: the path causes a visible security consequence. A proven finding needs the full six-kind chain: entrypoint -> reachability -> control -> guard -> sink -> impact.

Severity (use when accepting an impact-class fact): CRITICAL (remote code execution, device takeover); HIGH (high-value data disclosure, credential theft, code execution in app process); MEDIUM (real bounded impact requiring local app or interaction); LOW (fragile/limited impact); IGNORED (unreachable, non-security, crash-only).

Rejection rules — reject when ANY hold: evidence is inline-only/speculative with no manifest or decompiled-source trace (decx output); the exact sink path is rebuilt from trusted constants; dangerous fields are stripped before the sink; a non-bypassable guard covers the exact method; impact is only crash/compatibility with no security consequence.
prompt.instructions: Apply the four-element gate (reachable/controllable/deeply-traced/impactful) and reject facts with inline-only evidence.
